Notifications

📧 Email setup (EmailJS — free)

Sign up free at emailjs.com, connect your Gmail/Outlook, and paste the 3 keys below. Takes ~5 minutes once.

Your EmailJS template should accept variables: to_email, subject, message.

💬 WhatsApp setup (CallMeBot — free)

Each recipient sends I allow callmebot to send me messages to +34 644 51 95 23 on WhatsApp, gets an API key back, and enters it below against their number.

👥 Recipients
NameEmailWhatsAppTriggers
📎 Report attachments (local download)

This app has no server, so it can't send a file straight to someone's phone or inbox. With this on, the report instead downloads to your own Downloads folder the moment you submit — the notification text tells you the filename so you remember to forward it yourself.

🧪 Test

Send a test notification for each trigger type to everyone subscribed to it.

⚠ Payments with no Schedule tag

These payments never had a Schedule selected, so they're invisible in the Dashboard's per-phase totals and the Schedule Summary Report. Assign each one to its correct phase, or select several and bulk-assign at once.

DateVendorCategoryDescription Amount ₹StatusAssign Schedule
⚠ Possible duplicate payments

Same vendor, same amount, same category, entered on more than one date — usually means a rejected or held payment was re-typed the next day instead of just being resubmitted. Keep the entry that's actually correct and delete the rest.

These actions cannot be undone. Use only when you're sure — for example, clearing test/demo data before starting real entries.

Add Line Item to Rate Card
If this item has a fixed allowance for the 09–21 shift, enter it here. Leave blank to use default 1.5 CS calculation.
CS multiplier when no fixed allowance defined. Default 1.5.
Variable Rate (rate changes every day)
When checked, the rate field will be editable each time you log an estimate or actual for this item — e.g. Lights, Fuel, Generators

Production Finance — Vyjayanthi Movies
Connect your data file
All your data lives in k2_data.json — separate from this app file.
Open the same file every time. Share it with your team via WhatsApp, shared drive, or email.
Load your data
For full file saving, open in Chrome or Edge.
You can still import a previously exported JSON file or start fresh.
Chrome / Edge · Data never leaves your computer · No internet needed after first load

📋 Manual Topsheet Builder

Pick items from the rate card — rates and legends are auto-pulled. Enter counts and CS to auto-calculate amounts. Generates a topsheet and posts to actuals.

Set rehearsal days — shoot days auto-calculate from total
+ Extra days beyond shoot period (setwork, lighting etc.)
Extra days will be added as separate line items at the same daily rate
Add from Rate Card
🕐 Crew Timings (from Call Sheet)
Shows per-department call/pack times from the call sheet. Edit to override for this topsheet. Added as notes on the printout.
Topsheet Lines
Line ItemCategory Rate ₹ Type Count CS Calc ₹ Override ₹ Allow. ₹ Total ₹
Fixed Allowance = per-person amount NOT multiplied by CS (e.g. conveyance, food allowance)

📝 Quick Bill Entry

Create a full bill record manually. Posts to actuals and links to payments like a scanned bill.

Have a physical bill or image? — AI will extract details automatically (or show side-by-side entry if blocked)

Edit Artist

📋 Paste Payments from Excel

Copy rows from Excel and paste below. Expected column order (tab-separated, matching a normal Excel copy):
Date · Vendor · Category · Sub-Category · Description · Invoice Date · Due Date · Amount · Paid? (Y/N) · UTR · Pay Date
Dates can be in DD/MM/YYYY, DD-MM-YYYY, or YYYY-MM-DD. Category should match one of the existing dropdown options — if it doesn't, it's still imported but flagged for you to check. Sub-Category, UTR, and Pay Date are optional — leave the cell blank if not applicable.

🧾 Generate Topsheet from Actors Day Log

Pick a person and date range — every entry already logged for them under that name gets pulled together into one payment, with the full day-by-day breakdown kept in the notes.
Misc Charges (optional)
Topsheet

🧩 Flexible Multi-Role Topsheet

For crews with multiple tiers (Head / 1st Asst / 2nd Asst / Riggers etc.) — or for equipment hire across many days (Rostrum, Black Cloth, Lights, Camera, Crane etc.). Link each line item to the Rate Card to auto-fill rate — just enter the count (headcount for crew, quantity for hire). Generate a row per date from a range, or add rows manually for split shifts.

📅 Generate Date Rows from Range
Auto-fills Call/Pack/CS/Day Type from Call Sheets where available. Existing rows are kept — new dates are appended.
Line Items — crew tiers (Head, 1st Asst…) or hire items (Rostrum, Lights, Camera…) — link to Rate Card for auto rate
Link a line item to a Rate Card item to auto-fill its Rate ₹ on every row. For crew, "Count" is headcount; for hire (equipment), "Count" is quantity hired — set the rate type to Per Day so it's rate × quantity, not multiplied by call sheet. Leave unlinked to enter rate manually per row.
🍱 Mess Bills / Food Expenses
to
Dates are rows, categories are columns (Production, Snacks, Consumables…). Add a label under a date's picker for one-off variants like "26/02 Night Dinner". Preset category names are saved and reused next time. Any cell or the Row Total can take a formula too, e.g. type 5*2000 and it'll evaluate to 10,000.
🧾 Production Expenses
to
Dates are rows, items are columns — click an item name to rename it. Each item has Qty/Heads · Call Sheets · Rate, with Amount auto-computed as Qty × Rate × (Call Sheets, or 1 if left blank). Grand Total sums every item for that date.
🔧 Hires
to
Items are rows (Truss Hire, Rostrum Hire, Haze/Smoke Machine Hire…), dates are columns. Each item has one fixed Rate that applies across every date — only Qty varies per date. Amount = Qty × Rate.

🔗 Link Actuals to Estimate Lines

Each entry below must be linked to an estimate line item so actuals appear correctly on the topsheet. Select the matching estimate line for each row, then click Save.

⚠ Unlinked Adhoc Entries (from previous bills)
